title: Latest News from Science Magazine
description: Keyword search result for Alerts: News Weekly Headlines -- New results matching your keyword search query (updated daily).

items.title: How can you tell whether your dog is right-pawed or left-pawed?
items.description: <cite>Science</cite> chats with researchers about a new method of assessing canine “handedness” and what it reveals about your pup’s health and personality

items.title: ‘Working without them is painful’: WHO’s Ebola chief reflects on U.S. colleagues’ absence
items.description: Chikwe Ihekweazu, on the ground in Congo, discusses challenges of bringing the outbreak under control

items.title: Canadian biologists decry plans to bypass environmental reviews for infrastructure projects
items.description: Government proposal to speed up development could harm protected plants and animals, scientists say

items.title: Misconduct sleuth in China swiftly gains acclaim for calling out questionable papers
items.description: Independent vlogger Geng Hongwei galvanizes often-sluggish institutions to investigate and punish prominent scientists

items.title: NSF imposes stricter conflict-of-interest rules for grant-review panels
items.description: Researchers say changes could further burden the understaffed agency

items.title: Frozen squirrel poop rewrites rodent evolution, reveals new details about mammoths
items.description: DNA from ground squirrel coprolites offers an unusually detailed record of ice age ecosystems

items.title: Diabetes society officials resign after removal of Trump protesters from conference
items.description: As online support for the experts spreads, ADA members have resigned from their positions

items.title: NIH plans to cap number of grants a scientist can have at once
items.description: Agency is bringing back a version of a funding limit that was quickly abandoned nearly a decade ago

items.title: Leading supplier of research monkeys declares bankruptcy
items.description: Facing economic and regulatory pressures, Inotiv says it is seeking ways to continue operations

items.title: How did so many theropod dinosaurs come to have tiny arms?
items.description: Shrunken forelimbs were likely a byproduct of large, powerful skulls—with one notable exception
